channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ramos' two home runs and 5 RBIs lead Giants to 10-1 victory over Blue Jays
item.description: In a dominant performance, New York Yankees' star player Ramos delivered a standout game with two home runs and five RBIs, propelling the Giants to a decisive 10-1 win against the Blue Jays. The game,...

item.title: Landslide in Kerala's Wayanad kills one, triggers red alert amid heavy rain
item.description: A red alert was issued in Kerala's Wayanad district after a landslide at the Kalladi tunnel project site killed one person and triggered rescue operations. The incident occurred around 11 am, promptin...

item.title: 58 Students Face Consequences for Academic Dishonesty Involving ChatGPT
item.description: Fifty-eight students have had their examination results nullified after being found guilty of academic dishonesty, including the unauthorized use of ChatGPT, in a move that highlights growing concerns...

item.title: Trump's alleged interference in World Cup match sparks global controversy
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding Donald Trump's alleged interference in a World Cup match between the USA and Belgium. Trump claimed to have personally intervened in three phone calls...

item.title: Nigeria Police Hunt for Adeyemi Over Fake Agency Scandal
item.description: The Nigerian police are actively searching for Adeniyi Adeyemi, the disgraced director general of the fake agency PFIPC, following allegations of fraud and misuse of public resources. The scandal erup...

item.title: Kenya Police Stop Youth Protest Near Harambee House During Saba Saba Restrictions
item.description: Kenyan police in plain clothes arrested several youths near Harambee House and Jogoo House on Tuesday morning as they tried to gather for Saba Saba commemorations. The arrests happened before the yout...

item.title: Mike Phillips' Leadership Offers Hope for Gilas Amid Challenges
item.description: The article discusses how Mike Phillips' energetic and positive attitude is providing a spark of optimism for the Gilas Pilipinas basketball team, despite facing numerous challenges. FIBA technical di...

item.title: Public Protector Releases Findings on Cape Town Municipal Services Investigation
item.description: The Public Protector South Africa (PPSA) has concluded a systemic investigation into allegations that the City of Cape Town failed to provide basic municipal services in the communities of Langa Flats...

item.title: Damascus blasts during Macron's visit injure 18
item.description: Two bombs exploded near the hotel in Damascus where French President Emmanuel Macron held meetings during his visit to Syria, injuring 18 people including four police officers. The blasts occurred in ...

item.title: Kenyan Journalist Escapes Abduction After Criticizing President
item.description: Alex Kiprotich, associate editor of The Standard newspaper, narrowly avoided abduction by armed men after criticizing President William Ruto. The incident occurred three days after Ruto attacked The S...

item.title: Mpumalanga Hawks secure preservation order for Toyota Hilux found with concealed dagga
item.description: The Hawks in Mpumalanga have obtained a preservation order for a Toyota Hilux valued at approximately R180,000 after police discovered four parcels of dagga concealed in the vehicle during a stop-and-...

item.title: Senate Impeachment Court Requests Testimonies from OVP and NBI Officials in Vice President Duterte's Trial
item.description: The House prosecution panel has formally requested the Senate impeachment court to allow the presentation of Office of the Vice President (OVP) chief of staff Zuleika Lopez and National Bureau of Inve...

item.title: Belgium Advances in World Cup 2026 as Ronaldo's Career Ends with Spain's Win
item.description: Belgium secured a dominant 4-1 victory over the USA in their World Cup 2026 clash, eliminating the Americans from the tournament and advancing to the quarter-finals. The match, held in Seattle, saw Be...

item.title: OPay Introduces New Security Features to Enhance User Protection
item.description: OPay has rolled out two advanced security tools: Emergency Lock and Safety PIN, aimed at safeguarding users' digital transactions. These features empower customers to take immediate action against pot...

item.title: Letters Discuss Economic Challenges and Policy Criticisms
item.description: The article compiles letters to the editor addressing Canada's economic struggles, including stagnant growth, rising food prices, and mounting debt. Contributors criticize Prime Minister Justin Trudea...

item.title: Rise in e-scooter and e-bike injuries leads to £110m in payouts and higher insurance premiums
item.description: The cost of damages paid to individuals injured by e-scooters and e-bikes in the UK has surpassed £110m, according to BBC reports. This figure, which includes the largest individual payout of £20m, ha...

item.title: Tanzanian Businessman Bosco Chuwa Dies Suddenly, Family Provides Details
item.description: The family of Tanzanian businessman Bosco Chuwa has shared details about his unexpected death, which has shocked the business community. Chuwa, a respected entrepreneur with diverse business interests...

item.title: European Conservative Leaders Defend Giorgia Meloni After Trump's Public Criticism
item.description: European conservative figures are urging President Donald Trump to stop publicly attacking Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, arguing that she remains one of his strongest ideological allies in Eu...

item.title: Malawian Nationals Camp Outside Consulate Amid Repatriation Crisis in South Africa
item.description: In Johannesburg, the Malawian consulate's parking lot has become an impromptu shelter for repatriates seeking to return home amid escalating anti-foreigner tensions. Since the outbreak of unrest in ea...

item.title: India's Largest Private Earth Observation Satellite Faces Communication Loss Due to Solar Storm
item.description: Mission Drishti, developed by Bengaluru-based startup GalaxEye, is India's largest privately built Earth observation satellite. Launched on May 3, 2026, aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg, Calif...
